privileged_info_summary: Verified that the task operates entirely on synthetic data, as the EarthMapper-3 requirements document is generated natively within the setup script. Orbital mechanics calculations confirm that a 16-day/233-rev sun-synchronous orbit necessitates an SMA of ~7077.7 km and an inclination of ~98.19 degrees, which sit perfectly within the verification tolerances. However, multiple critical contradictions exist between the explicit requirements given to the agent and the metadata used to score them: while the agent is instructed to maintain longitude drift within 1.0 degree and RAAN rate at 0.9856 +/- 0.05 deg/day, the verifier relies on arbitrarily wider metadata thresholds of 2.0 degrees and [0.93, 1.04] deg/day respectively.
